name: Circular gravel between Teruel and Albarracín
name_original: Circular gravel entre Teruel y Albarracín
description:
The growing fame of the award-winning Empty Mountains trip is putting this wonderful and uninhabited Aragonese territory of Teruel on the cycling map.

This Route proposes you, leaving the city of Teruel, to know some parts of the official route of the Empty Mountains route, spinning a circular itinerary that will take you to visit the town of Albarracín, a National Monument and proposed by UNESCO as a World Heritage Site. .

You will cross a good number of spectacular landscapes: the area of the Arcillas de Teruel, with its amazing colorful formations, the protected landscape of the Pinares de Rodeno and its dense forests, and part of the steep and ancient Sierra de Albarracín.

It is a comfortable route and perfect for gravel bikes. It is ideal to do it quietly in two stages, sleeping and enjoying the hospitality and culinary quality of the town of Albarracín. In total you will pedal about 77 kilometers and you will add 1200 meters of positive elevation gain.

The Route begins and ends at the Teruel railway station, a point that you can reach by train and which also has a large car park where you can also leave your vehicle during the days you spend pedaling in the area.

Since you are here, do not hesitate to visit both the historic and charming city of Teruel and the incredible town of Albarracín and the views from its walls at sunset.

Any time of the year is good to do this Route, as long as we have the proper preparation and equipment; although it is at the end of summer when you can enjoy the route the most, with pleasant temperatures and long days.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Teruel offers diverse and wild landscapes, characterized by vast mountainous terrain, high elevation plateaus, and extensive forest roads. The region features striking red sandstone formations, deep river valleys, and unique semi-desert clay lands. This varied geography provides a wide range of off-road experiences for gravel cyclists. Best gravel bike trails around Teruel The most popular gravel bike trail is Circular gravel between Teruel and Albarracín, a 47.6 miles (76.6 km) trail that takes about 7 hours 18 minutes to complete. This route connects two significant locations through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Water Erosion – The Clays of Teruel loop from Teruel, a difficult 61.1 miles (98.3 km) path. This route explores the unique semi-desert landscapes and spectacular clay formations near Teruel city. Local gravel bikers also love the Pico Javalambre – Viaducto de Peñaflores loop from La Puebla de Valverde, a 59.9 miles (96.6 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 9 hours 33 minutes. Gravel biking around Teruel is defined by vast mountainous landscapes, extensive forest roads, and unique semi-desert clay lands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Teruel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 4 reviews. More than 100 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Teruel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in Teruel?
A: Teruel offers a wide selection of gravel biking routes, with over 20 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, predominantly featuring difficult and moderate options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails in Teruel?
A: Teruel's gravel trails traverse incredibly diverse landscapes. You'll encounter vast mountainous terrain, high elevation plateaus, and extensive forest roads, particularly in areas like the Serranía Celtibérica. The region also features striking red sandstone formations near Albarracín, deep river valleys, and unique semi-desert clay lands, offering a varied off-road experience.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the gravel bike trails in Teruel?
A: Many gravel routes in Teruel pass by significant natural features. You can explore unique geological formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Clays of Teruel&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy serene waters at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arquillo de San Blas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider routes that lead to viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5376480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cruz (Peña Botadera) Lookout&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical towns or cultural sites accessible by gravel bike?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history and culture. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e825011515/circular-gravel-between-teruel-and-albarracin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular gravel between Teruel and Albarracín&lt;/a&gt;, connect you to charming towns. Albarracín is consistently ranked among Spain's prettiest villages, known for its historic center. Teruel city itself boasts UNESCO World Heritage Mudejar architecture, including La Torre de El Salvador and the Teruel Cathedral.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Teruel?
A: Teruel offers excellent gravel biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for riding. Summers can be hot, especially in lower elevations, but higher mountainous areas offer cooler alternatives. Winters can bring cold temperatures and snow, particularly in the higher Sierras, so checking local weather conditions is advisable.

Q: Are there circular gravel routes available in Teruel?
A: Yes, many of the gravel routes in Teruel are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e825011515/circular-gravel-between-teruel-and-albarracin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular gravel between Teruel and Albarracín&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre – Viaducto de Peñaflores loop from La Puebla de Valverde&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging gravel routes for experienced riders?
A: Teruel is well-suited for experienced gravel bikers, with a significant number of difficult routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Erosion – The Clays of Teruel loop from Teruel&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 61.1-mile path exploring unique semi-desert landscapes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre – Viaducto de Peñaflores loop from La Puebla de Valverde&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial elevation gain.

Q: Is parking available near the gravel bike trails in Teruel?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and villages that serve as starting points for many gravel routes, such as Teruel city, Albarracín, and La Puebla de Valverde. Given the region's low population density, finding parking is usually not a major issue, especially in smaller communities.

Q: Can I access gravel bike trails in Teruel using public transport?
A: While Teruel is known for its remote character, the capital city of Teruel has some public transport connections. However, reaching the more secluded trailheads often requires personal transport. It's best to plan your route and check specific public transport options to the starting points of your chosen trails.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near the gravel trails?
A: Yes, Teruel's river valleys and canyons offer scenic beauty and opportunities for refreshing swims. Rivers like the Tajo and Guadalope carve through the landscape, providing potential spots for wild swimming, especially during warmer months. Always exercise caution and respect local regulations when considering wild swimming.

Q: What do other gravel bikers say about the trails in Teruel?
A: The gravel biking experience in Teruel is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 4 reviews. Over 100 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore the region, often praising the diverse terrain, the sense of wilderness, and the extensive network of forest roads that allow for true off-road adventures.
tips:
This picturesque medieval town, located in the province of Teruel, is transformed when the sun goes down. The cobbled streets and houses with whimsical architecture take on a special charm under the light of the moon and the street lamps. 
Walking through Albarracín at night is like travelling back in time. The historical monuments, such as the Cathedral and the walls, are illuminated, creating a magical and romantic atmosphere. In addition, during the summer months, you can enjoy guided night tours like the one we do that allow you to discover the secrets and legends of this charming place. A visit is totally recommended. 🚶‍♀️🌒😊
Just enjoy the feeling of floating on the bike while you cross these magnificent landscapes.
Here you can see what incredible power nature has, but at the same time so beautiful..

waypoints.name: Gravel track in the Paisaje Protegido de los Pinares de Rodeno
